_meta.comment: English message bundle. Mirrors cs.json key-for-key.

Footer.mixedLanguageNote: Some content (author's notes, location names, place descriptions) may appear in Czech.

Home.disclaimerHeading: A small apology at the end
Home.disclaimerApology: If you searched one of the listed locations and found no four-leaf clover — I'm truly sorry 😇. I was probably faster and it's already in the collection.
Home.disclaimerOffer: Would you like a four-leaf clover for luck — for yourself or for someone close to you? Reach out on LinkedIn and we'll sort out the details. (And if we know each other in person, feel free to write wherever we usually do. 🙂)
Home.disclaimerTagline: You can never have too much luck — and it should be given away for free :)

Statistiky.metaDescription: The four-leaf clover collection at a glance.
Statistiky.deviationSubtitle: Deviation: for a location with a polygon, a 🍀 outside the polygon (any distance); for a polygon-less location, more than {radius} m from the centre. Only non-anonymized 🍀 with GPS assigned to a location are counted.
Statistiky.deviationNone: No deviated 🍀 — every GPS sits inside its location.
Statistiky.deviationShowFindsTitle: Show this location's 🍀 in the collection
Statistiky.deviationHelpButtonAria: Help — how deviated finds are computed
Statistiky.deviationHelpIntro: A deviated 🍀 is one whose GPS doesn't sit where the location says it should. What's counted and how:
Statistiky.deviationHelpRule1: Location with a polygon: a 🍀 is deviated when it lies outside the polygon (AOI) — any distance.
Statistiky.deviationHelpRule2: Polygon-less location: a 🍀 is deviated when it's more than {radius} m from the GPS centre.
Statistiky.deviationHelpRule3: Only non-anonymized 🍀 that have GPS and are assigned to a location are counted.
Statistiky.deviationHelpMeasure1: Distance: for a location with a polygon, the great-circle distance from the polygon edge; for a polygon-less location, the distance from the centre (the same metric as on the 🍀 detail).
Statistiky.deviationHelpMeasure2: The median is more robust to a few outliers than the mean.
Statistiky.deviationHelpMeasure3: Direction: the azimuth from the location's GPS centre to the 🍀, rounded to 8 compass points (the rose).
Statistiky.deviationHelpProb1: Probability = the share of deviated among all counted 🍀; "≈ 1 in N" is its reciprocal.
Statistiky.deviationHelpProb2: Location with the highest probability: the highest deviated ratio, only locations with at least 10 🍀; anonymized locations are not listed.
Statistiky.deviationHelpProb3: Most deviated 🍀 = the single 🍀 with the largest deviation distance.
Statistiky.distanceFromDefaultTitle: Distance from the GPS centre of the default location map #00001
Statistiky.timePaceSummary: sum of {sessions, plural, one {# session} other {# sessions}} of clover hunting
Statistiky.timePaceSummaryAt: at {count, plural, one {# location} other {# locations}}
Statistiky.timePaceTitle: A session = consecutive 🍀 at the same location with ≤ 15 min gap. Each session gets a 2 min baseline (time before the first 🍀).
Statistiky.paceYearIncompleteFirst: First year of collecting — counted from the first 🍀, not from 1 January. Pace is based on {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}.
Statistiky.paceYearIncompleteLast: Year in progress — counted up to today, not to 31 December. Pace is based on {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}.
Statistiky.paceYearIncompleteOnly: Partial year — from the first 🍀 to today, not a full calendar year. Pace is based on {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}.
Statistiky.yearTimeSpentTitle: Estimated picking time in this year — sessions + 2 min baseline / session.
Statistiky.yearAvgPerSessionTitle: Average 🍀 per session in this year.
Statistiky.geoSubtitle: Country is determined from the GPS centre of the location (point-in-polygon against Natural Earth borders), city from the cadastral area. The city overview also counts anonymized and former locations; the country/region map plots only locations with public GPS.
Statistiky.calendarSubtitle: Distribution of 🍀 by hour of day, day of week, month of year, by year and by day of the calendar year.
Statistiky.heatmapSubtitle: 🍀 counts by day of year across all years. Green saturation reflects the share against the busiest day ({max}).
Statistiky.streakHeading: Longest streak of days with a found 🍀
Statistiky.streakDays: {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} in a row
Statistiky.minuteHeatmapSubtitle: 🍀 counts by day of year (vertical, Jan 1 at top) and by minute of day (horizontal, 0:00 on the left). Aggregated across all years. Green saturation reflects the 🍀 count in each cell.
Statistiky.minuteHeatmapEmpty: No 🍀 with a timestamp to display.
Statistiky.distanceSubtitle: Logarithmic split by great-circle distance from the GPS centre of the default location map. Anonymized 🍀 and 🍀 without GPS are excluded.
Statistiky.jubileeSubtitle: Special numbers (111, 1111, 11111, 666, 6666) and every thousandth 🍀 in the collection.
Statistiky.recordJubileeTitle: Czech record — the largest four-leaf clover collection in Czechia
Statistiky.topByDensitySubtitle: Most clovers per 100 m² (≥ 10 🍀; polygon-less locations counted as a 5 m-radius circle)
Statistiky.topBySessionsSubtitle: Where I went clover-hunting most often (trip count and average per trip)
Statistiky.topAvgDensityTitle: Average density per location (clovers / 100 m², across locations with at least one 🍀)
Statistiky.topAvgSessionsTitle: Average 🍀 per single trip (across all collecting sessions)
Statistiky.rowFindsAria: Show the location's finds on the Collection page

FindDetail.noLocation: No location is assigned to this find.
FindDetail.distanceTitle: Great-circle distance from the GPS centre of the default location map #00001
FindDetail.offsetTitlePolygon: Great-circle distance from the location polygon edge (0 = inside the AOI)
FindDetail.offsetTitleCenter: Great-circle distance from the location map's GPS centre
FindDetail.mapMarkerOutside: The find's GPS lies outside the area captured by this location map.
FindDetail.mapMarkerNoGps: The find has no recorded GPS coordinates — its position can't be drawn on the map.
FindDetail.mapStatusInPolygon: Find is inside the location polygon.
FindDetail.mapStatusInMapFromCenter: Find is inside the location's map area ({distance} from its centre).
FindDetail.mapStatusOutsidePolygon: Find is outside the location polygon ({distance} from the edge).
FindDetail.mapStatusOutsideRadius: Find is outside the {radius} m radius around the location centre ({distance} from the centre).
FindDetail.mapStatusOutsideMap: Find is outside the displayed map.
FindDetail.mapStatusOutsideMapFromCenter: Find is outside the displayed map ({distance} from its centre).
FindDetail.mapStatusOutsideMapFromPolygon: Find is outside the displayed map ({distance} from the polygon edge).

SbirkaHelp.buttonTitle: Help — how to use the Collection page
SbirkaHelp.buttonAria: Open help for the Collection page
SbirkaHelp.intro: The Collection is a searchable catalogue of every recorded four-leaf clover. You can sort and filter it, vote for your favourite, and open the detail page with photos and position.
SbirkaHelp.sectionFilters1: The "Search" field looks up the find number (e.g. 19211), notes and location name.
SbirkaHelp.sectionFilters2: The "Country", "City" and "Location" filters narrow by geography and cascade together (picking a location fills in its country and city too). "State" now lets you pick several states at once — e.g. Lost + Anonymized shows finds that are both. "Year" limits to a specific year.
SbirkaHelp.sectionFilters3: Every filter option shows, in brackets, how many finds it matches — a number that reacts to the other active filters. Options that would return nothing drop out of the list.
SbirkaHelp.sectionFilters4: The "With donation photo" toggle shows only finds whose new owner sent a photo after the gift; "Hide largest location" drops finds from the dominant location. The number on each reflects how many finds it touches under the current filter.
SbirkaHelp.sectionFilters5: When any filter is active, an "Filter is active — N 🍀 …" line above the results spells out what you're filtering by (handy after arriving from another page, e.g. "Peak day"). Filter settings survive a language switch.
SbirkaHelp.sectionViews1: "Tiles" show big thumbnails with metadata. "List" is compact with a location map on the right.
SbirkaHelp.sectionViews2: Sort: Newest (default), Oldest, Farthest from map #00001, Most popular (by votes).
SbirkaHelp.sectionViews3: Page size (finds per page) can be set from the toolbar.
SbirkaHelp.sectionMap1: When the filtered finds fit into a single location (or the result is one find), a "Show on map" button appears — it zooms the map to that location, enables the Finds layer, and notes in the location panel which filter you're viewing by. Anonymized or multi-location results have no button (they have no single point on the map).
SbirkaHelp.sectionMap2: Each row in the List view has a pin icon on the right — that opens the map directly on that specific find (with a pulsing highlight marker).
SbirkaHelp.sectionVote1: Click the thumbs-up to vote for your favourite find. Voting is anonymous, one vote per device.
SbirkaHelp.sectionVote2: The Top 10 most popular finds live on the Statistics page.
SbirkaHelp.sectionDetail1: Clicking a row or tile opens the find detail — larger photos, GPS, notes, location and optionally galleries of donation or free photos.

LokalityHelp.buttonTitle: Help — how to use the Locations page
LokalityHelp.buttonAria: Open help for the Locations page
LokalityHelp.intro: Locations are places where four-leaf clovers were searched for. Each one has its own location map (polygon or point), find statistics, and may have its own real photo of the place.
LokalityHelp.sectionFilters1: "Search" looks up the location id, code, description and cadastral area.
LokalityHelp.sectionFilters2: "City" filters by cadastral area. "Country" filters by the location's GPS centre.
LokalityHelp.sectionFilters3: The "With photo" toggle shows only locations that have their own real photo of the place (separate from the location map).
LokalityHelp.sectionSort1: Most finds (default), Newest finds, By code (alphabetical), Distance from map #00001 (ascending / descending).
LokalityHelp.sectionAnonGone1: Anonymized locations (sensitive places) are hidden by default. The "Show anonymized" toggle reveals them, but their code and description stay private.
LokalityHelp.sectionAnonGone2: Former locations (the NEEXISTUJE- prefix, e.g. "NEEXISTUJE-ZLÍN_…") are places that no longer physically exist (built over, terrain change). "Show former" adds them; "Only former" filters down to just those.
LokalityHelp.sectionExpand1: Clicking a location row expands a statistics panel: total finds, first and last find, state breakdown, yearly distribution.
LokalityHelp.sectionDetail1: The location card links to the location-map detail — polygon, AOI, GPS centre and all related finds.

MapaHelp.buttonTitle: Help — how to use the Map page
MapaHelp.buttonAria: Open help for the Map page
MapaHelp.intro: The map shows every location (as polygons) and every find with GPS (as dots). Anonymized locations are not visible on the map.
MapaHelp.sectionLayers1: "Locations" are polygons of the location maps — active ones in blue, parent locations (those with sub-parts) in grey. Clicking a polygon or a row in the sidebar zooms in.
MapaHelp.sectionLayers2: "Finds" are the ~17,000 small dots (every single find with GPS). Click a dot to open a popup with the find number, thumbnail and link to detail.
MapaHelp.sectionLayers3: "Former" (opt-in) shows the NEEXISTUJE- prefixed locations as red striped polygons.
MapaHelp.sectionLayers4: Under "Finds" the "Hide deviated" sub-toggle drops dots outside the location polygon, or more than 5 m from the centre for polygon-less locations. The same threshold drives the three-band (green/amber/red) offset colours on the collection list and the find detail — amber = outside the polygon but inside one of the location-map image bboxes, red = outside every map. You can now turn those same colours on for the map dots too via the "Colour-code deviations" toggle (on by default). When you pick a specific location in the sidebar, the counts on both toggles narrow to that location's finds.
MapaHelp.sectionLayers5: The "Icon size" slider makes the find dots on the map larger or smaller (0.6–2×).
MapaHelp.sectionLayers6: Toggle state and icon size are saved in your browser, so the next visit starts where you left off.
MapaHelp.sectionNavigation1: Zoom: mouse wheel or pinch on touchpad / mobile. Pan: mouse drag or finger drag.
MapaHelp.sectionNavigation2: At higher zoom each location's detailed PNG overlay appears in the background (OpenStreetMap tiles + AOI).
MapaHelp.sectionSidebar1: The sidebar on the right (a bottom sheet on mobile) lets you search by id, code or name. Clicking a row zooms to that location.
MapaHelp.sectionDeepLinks1: When you reach the map from a find detail or from the "Show on map" button in the Collection, the map auto-zooms to that find or location and highlights the matching dots.
MapaHelp.sectionDeepLinks2: When the Collection filter is something other than location (a state, a year, a country, etc.), the button is deliberately missing — the results would be scattered across the world and the map wouldn't be useful.

Privacy.metaDescription: What personal data Čtyřlístkotéka processes, why, for how long, and what rights you have.
Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: Čtyřlístkotéka is a non-commercial showcase of a private four-leaf clover collection. We collect as little about you as possible — the site uses no advertising and no third-party tracking. Below is an overview of what is nonetheless processed.
Privacy.controllerText: The data controller is the operator of Čtyřlístkotéka. For any data-protection matter, contact us at <email>safronus@gmail.com</email>.
Privacy.dataHeading: 2. What data we process, and why
Privacy.analyticsText: We measure traffic with our own GoatCounter instance running on our server (stats.ctyrlistkoteka.cz). It uses no cookies and does not profile you; your IP address is processed only transiently, as a hashed fingerprint to distinguish unique visits, and then discarded. No data is shared with any third party. Purpose: a basic overview of traffic. Legal basis: legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).
Privacy.securityText: The web server records request IP addresses in operational logs; the fail2ban tool uses them to block attacks. Purpose: protecting the site from abuse. Legal basis: legitimate interest. Logs are rotated and deleted regularly.
Privacy.votingText: When you vote on a find, we store a functional cookie with a random identifier and a fingerprint derived from your IP address, browser and language (a one-way hash with a server-side secret). It serves only to stop you voting on the same find more than once. We do not store your actual IP address — you cannot be identified from a database dump. Legal basis: legitimate interest (protection against vote manipulation).
Privacy.mapText: On the Map page, map tiles are loaded from the OpenStreetMap Foundation's servers, which sends them your IP address and browser details needed to deliver the tiles. This processing is governed by the <osm>OpenStreetMap Foundation privacy policy</osm>. If you would rather avoid it, do not open the Map page.
Privacy.cookiesIntro: The site stores nothing on your device for advertising or cross-site tracking. It uses only:
Privacy.cookieVoteDesc: a functional cookie (1-year lifetime), set only when you vote, preventing double voting.
Privacy.cookieViewDesc: a functional cookie (1-year lifetime) remembering whether you prefer the tile grid or the list in the Collection. Not used for tracking.
Privacy.storageThemeDesc: your appearance choice (light/dark/clover) stored in the browser's localStorage; it is never sent to the server.
Privacy.cookiesNoBanner: Because we use no tracking or advertising cookies, the site does not require a consent (“cookie”) banner under § 89 of Czech Act No. 127/2005 Coll.
Privacy.retentionText: Visitor statistics are kept in aggregate form. Operational logs and fail2ban records are rotated and deleted regularly. Vote records are kept while the voting feature is active, or until an erasure request is handled.
Privacy.recipientsHeading: 5. Recipients and transfers outside the EU
Privacy.recipientsText: The site and its database run on a server provided by OVH in a data centre in France (EU), acting as a processor. Apart from OpenStreetMap map tiles (see above), we pass data to no other third party. No personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA.
Privacy.rightsText: You have the right to access your data, to rectify or erase it, to restrict processing, to data portability, and to object to processing based on legitimate interest. To exercise a request, write to <email>safronus@gmail.com</email>. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority — the <uoou>Czech Data Protection Authority (Úřad pro ochranu osobních údajů)</uoou>.
Privacy.contentText: Notes on finds may occasionally mention other people. For finds marked as anonymized, the site shows no notes, no precise location and no names, and such finds are excluded from search engines.
Privacy.changesText: We may update this policy as the site evolves. The current version is always on this page, with the effective date shown at the top.
